creator: edwin@mavetju.org
text:
On Thu, Sep 27, 2007 at 05:25:05AM +0000, pav@FreeBSD.org wrote:
> Synopsis: [patch] bsd.port.mk - INSTALL_KLD
> 
> State-Changed-From-To: open->feedback
> State-Changed-By: pav
> State-Changed-When: Thu Sep 27 05:22:15 UTC 2007
> State-Changed-Why: 
> Why can't INSTALL_SCRIPT be used? They look identical.

They look identifical, but they are not.

INSTALL_SCRIPT is for installing executable scripts.
INSTALL_KLD is for installing kernel modules.

I don't mind if they are put to be the same internally (i.e.
INSTALL_KLD=${INSTALL_SCRIPT}), but from the port-maintainers'
perspective the difference should be there. Just because it has
been working with INSTALL_PROGRAM on i386 and it now doesn't work
because of the STRIP command in it, that's why a different command
should be given for it.

Edwin
